title: Indulgent Jamaican Cucumber Paella
description: A lightly seasoned, low‑sodium paella featuring Persian cucumber and katsuramuki, perfect for a date night.

ingredients.name: saffron threads, steeped in 2 tbsp hot water

instructions: Heat olive oil in a paella pan over medium heat until shimmering.
Add diced onions and minced garlic, sauté until onions translucent, about 3 minutes.
Stir in smoked paprika and turmeric, cooking until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add the rice, stirring to coat grains in the spice mixture, cook for 2 minutes.
Pour in the saffron‑infused water, then add low‑sodium vegetable broth and diced tomatoes; bring to a gentle simmer, cover partially.
Reduce heat to low, let the rice absorb liquid for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
When rice is near cooked, gently fold in cucumber and katsuramuki slices, allowing them to warm through.
Remove the pan from heat, squeeze fresh lime juice over the paella, and let rest for 5 minutes.
Garnish with chopped herbs if desired and serve hot.
Enjoy the fragrant, low‑sodium Jamaican cucumber paella.
